content.problemStatement:
Akash has his maths test tomorrow. He secretly found out one of the questions from the test.
As the test is online, he wants to write a code that solves the question. However, he is not good at coding and thus, wants Chef to help him with that.

Chef is given a function F(X)F(X)F(X) such that the condition:


F(X)=F(2⋅X)F(X) = F(2\cdot X)F(X)=F(2⋅X)

holds true for all XXX, where XXX is a **positive integer**.
For a given positive integer NNN, find the **maximum** number of **distinct** elements in the array [F(1),F(2),F(3),…,F(N)][F(1), F(2), F(3), \ldots, F(N)][F(1),F(2),F(3),…,F(N)].


Input Format

First line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, one integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the **maximum** number of **distinct** elements in the array [F(1),F(2),F(3),…,F(N)][F(1), F(2), F(3), \ldots, F(N)][F(1),F(2),F(3),…,F(N)].
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible number of distinct elements in the array [F(1), F(2), …, F(N)].

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=2, odd numbers ≤2 are {1}, count=1. For N=5, odd numbers ≤5 are {1,3,5}, count=3.

solution.approach: The condition F(X) = F(2·X) forces all numbers that differ only by a factor of a power of two to have the same value. Every positive integer can be written as X = 2^k * odd, where 'odd' is the odd part of X. Hence F depends only on this odd part. To maximise distinct values we assign a different value to each possible odd part. The odd parts that appear among 1…N are exactly the odd numbers ≤ N. Their count is ceil(N/2) = (N+1)//2. So the answer for each test case is (N+1)//2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
